content.problemStatement:
You are given N+1N+1N+1 boxes. The ithi_{th}ith​ box contains AiA_iAi​ balls. Balls from the same box are identical, while balls from different boxes are distinct.

You need to arrange all the balls in a row such that before the occurrence of any ball from the N+1N+1N+1-th box, at least one ball from each of the first NNN boxes appears.

Determine the number of valid arrangements satisfying this condition. Since the answer can be very large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains integers NNN.
The next line contains N+1N + 1N+1 space separated integers A1,A2,...,AN,AN+1A_1,A_2,...,A_N,A_{N+1}A1​,A2​,...,AN​,AN+1​.




Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing the number of valid arrangements modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 500500500.
